name: NYRR Open Run: Canarsie Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Canarsie Park serves the neighborhoods of Canarsie and nearby Flatlands, Mill Basin, Marine Park, and Bergen Beach. Home to baseball fields and cricket pitches, it’s bordered by the Belt Parkway and Paerdegat Basin.

name: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic: St. Marys Park
description: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic is your start to a healthier lifestyle. With guidance and support from NYRR coaches and local run club members, you’ll learn everything you need to take on your first mile.NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.St. Mary's Park offers a wonderful respite from the hustle and bustle of urban life. Located in the Mott Haven neighborhood in the South Bronx, the park features rolling hills, expanses of green grass, and grand old trees. The site was once part of the estate of Jonas Bronck (1600-43), for whom the Bronx is named. It is one of the largest parks in the area with a pool, playground, basketball courts, an indoor recreation center, picnic seating areas, and more. In the winter, the hills are perfect for sledding. St. Mary’s Park received $30 million from NYC’s Anchor Parks initiative, which will renovate the park’s ballfields, pathways, and amphitheater.

name: NYRR Open Run: Inwood Hill Park New Runner Clinic
description: NYRR Open Run New Runner Clinic is your start to a healthier lifestyle. With guidance and support from NYRR coaches and local run club members, you’ll learn everything you need to take on your first mile. NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Evidence of Inwood Hill Park’s formation exists as caves, valleys, and ridges left as the result of shifting glaciers. Evidence of its uninhabited state afterward remains as its forest and salt marsh (the last natural one in Manhattan), and evidence of its use by Native Americans continues to be discovered. Most of the park was largely untouched by wars and development. The park features a hiking trail and the Hudson River Bike Trail, and is one of the best places in NYC to spot a bald eagle. It also includes athletic fields, playgrounds, dog runs, and a barbecue area.The course is three loops on hilly trails and walkways through woods and along a salt marsh.
location_name: Seaman Avenue and Isham Street Entrance (in Inwood Hill Park)

name: NYRR Open Run: Soundview Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Called the “Gateway to the Bronx River,” 205-acre Soundview Park is located where the Bronx River opens into the East River. This urban green space is filled with grassy baseball and soccer fields, a cricket pitch, basketball courts, a running track, walking/biking paths, picnic fields, and more! With its extensive views of the water, Soundview Park celebrates the beauty of both the Bronx and East Rivers. The back part of our Open Run course has beautiful views of the Bronx River waterfront for participants to enjoy!
location_name: Entrance - Lafayette Avenue and Morrison Avenue (in Soundview Park)

Whether you are a beginner or looking for a challenge; this class for all fitness levels and modifications are always provided. Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups, get your heart pumping, and connect with people in your community!
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: CANCELED: Strong For Life Circuit Training
description: A welcoming exercise class focused on helping participants feel stronger, more mobile, and more energized in daily life. Class includes low-impact strength and endurance exercises, mobility training, functional fitness and balance work. Modifications will be provided for all levels of fitness.

name: City of Water Day: Soundview Park Guided Walk
description: Join NAC staff for an exploration of Soundview Park’s Trails. We’ll discuss plant identification and maritime woodlands.The Natural Areas Conservancy will host a guided walk on the trails at Soundview Park as part of City of Water Day in order to explore the trail and learn about our local ecosystem. Attendees will learn about plant id, maritime woodlands, and aquatic plantsIf you are attending, please fill out this volunteer waiver.All ages are welcome. Attendees under 18 will need to be accompanied by an adult.What you should bring:A water bottleWeather-appropriate clothingSturdy, closed-toe shoesInsect repellentLong pants and tall socks recommendedIf you have any questions about this event or would like to learn more about the Natural Areas Conservancy, please visit naturalareasnyc.org, @naturalareasnyc on Instagram or contact Community Engagement Coordinator, Melissa Rosenthal at melissa.rosenthal@naturalareasnyc.org.

name: City of Water Day at Kaiser Park
description: Join the Stewardship Team in celebrating City of Water Day at Kaiser Park! This is a large community event where many organizations will be tabling and other activities will be available. We will be leading a coastal cleanup as well as a seining demo. Come dressed in sturdy boots or shoes, long pants, and clothing that can get dirty. Space is limited and registration is required. Volunteers under 18 must be accompanied by a chaperone. To sign up a group of 5 or more volunteers, please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov.

name: City of Water Day at Sunset Cove Park
description: This City of Water Day, volunteer with the Stewardship team to protect coastal areas of Sunset Cove Park! Attendees will help us remove debris that has washed ashore, contributing to the ongoing resilience of Sunset Cove's coastline. Please come dressed in sturdy boots or closed-shoes, long pants and clothing that can get dirty. Please DO NOT wear Crocs, sandals or shorts. Space is limited and registration is required. Volunteers under 18 must be accompanied by a chaperone. To sign up a group of 10 or more volunteers, please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov.

name: Yoga in the Park
description: Stretch, breathe, and find your inner peace at Randall’s Island Park’s free outdoor yoga series. Guided by a beloved, local yoga instructor, participants will flow through a calming and grounding sequence against the backdrop of the uptown skyline. All levels and abilities are welcome. Bring a friend, family member or two, or your yoga-loving pet! No registration required—just show up ready to move! We’ll have water and a limited number of mats available to borrow, so please bring your own if possible or show up early to claim one. Our free yoga classes run weekly on Tuesdays from 6:30 – 7:30 pm from May 5 – September 1, and Saturdays from 10:00 – 11:00 am, July 11 – August 29. On Saturdays, stay after and join our dance class! Please check Instagram for the most updated schedule. Location: East River Picnic Area For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation, or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

name: City of Water Day with Friends of Inwood Hill Park
description: Now in its 19th year, City of Water Day is a region-wide day organized by Waterfront Alliance and New York–New Jersey Harbor & Estuary Program (HEP), to champion a climate-resilient New York and New Jersey harbor. Friends of Inwood Hill Park is joining the Waterfront Alliance for another year to bring waterside programming to Inwood Hill Park alongside some of our favorite partner organizations, including the Urban Park Rangers, BioBus, the American Museum of Natural History, Riverkeeper, the New York Public Library, Creative Art Works, and more! We will meet in front of the Inwood Hill Nature Center for a full day of activities, including nature walks, trash cleanups, and more, to celebrate the water that connects our neighborhood and Inwood Hill Park to the larger Hudson River community and beyond.

name: Trees Count at Asser Levy Park
description: Volunteer with NYC Parks for Trees Count 2025! At each event you will receive a tote bag with all the materials needed to count trees. Next, we'll provide a 30-minute training session that includes the basics of tree identification and how to measure and assess each tree. Using our Tree Census app, you’ll split up into different zones within the park to start counting trees! After completing your first training event, you are welcome to continue counting trees on your own in smaller parks across the city. For your comfort and safety, please bring water and wear closed-toe shoes to this program.

name: Beginner Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in Beginner Yoga, a class to be enjoyed by all. Experience a fun and light-hearted, beginner flow that will move you seamlessly from Sun Salutation to a final peak pose. Please bring a mat. The class meets on the lawn north of Sobelsohn Basketball Court.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Outdoor Fitness,Yoga & Pilates Classes

name: City of Water Day: Island Hopping Canoeing Excursion
description: Its City of Water Day! Spend an afternoon exploring the islands that surround the Orchard Beach estuary by canoe. Experienced paddlers only. Participants are chosen by lottery. Lottery registration opens on Monday July 13.
location_name: Canoe and Kayak Launch (in Pelham Bay Park)

name: LaBlast® Fitness
description: This dance fitness program is based on ballroom dances, including those seen on Dancing with the Stars. This workout in disguise blends dance patterns, fitness, and music to create a low impact workout suitable for all levels.

name: STRONG Nation®
description: STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more. *Participants must bring a lock and lock up all personal belongings.
location_name: Rock Climbing Wall Room (in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center)
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Salt Marsh Saturday: City of Water Day Seining in the Harlem River
description: Have you ever explored the Harlem River? Randall’s Island Park Alliance will host a free seining program on City of Water Day!Grab a pair of waders and help drag a seining net along the shore of the Harlem River to discover the amazing biodiversity in our waterways. Learn how to identify fish, crabs, and jellyfish. All equipment is provided, but wear clothes you don’t mind getting wet! You must be 12 years or older to seine, but all are welcome to participate in our shoreline activities.Now in its 19th year, City of Water Day — taking place this year on Saturday, July 25 — is a region-wide day organized by Waterfront Alliance and New York–New Jersey Harbor & Estuary Program (HEP), to champion a climate-resilient New York and New Jersey harbor.From 11 AM – 2 PM, our Natural Areas staff will assist you in bringing a 30-foot net into the river to find some unique species of plants and animals that call this amazing and dynamic ecosystem home!We will also have part of our beloved Salt Marsh brought to the beach! RIPA’s team will bring some of the area’s wildlife and teach you about the vital ecosystem that protects nearby areas from flooding and erosion and creates habitats for countless creatures.Location: Water’s Edge Garden Beach at The Rock DockFor inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.
location_name: Water's Edge Garden (in Randall's Island Park)

name: Dance Fitness
description: Join Shape Up NYC for a free dance fitness class every Saturday at West Harlem Piers Park! The instructor for this class will change week to week. This summer the following instructors will be featured: Donnette "Sonnia" McFarlane, Claudia Gomez, Taneka Teel, Beverly Brown, Carolina Morales, Yhajira Muniz, Masayo Kado, Nathalie Roussel, Regina Nicosia, Erica Zhang, and Alejandra Villadiego. This class is appropriate for all fitness levels.
location_name: 125th Street and Marginal Street (in West Harlem Piers)
secondary_categories: Fitness,Shape Up NYC,Outdoor Fitness,Dance Classes,Exercise Classes

name: Groove & Glow
description: Bringing good vibes and great movement this cardio dance class blends K-pop beats, funk grooves, and feel-good choreography to help you sweat, smile, and glow. All levels welcome—just bring your energy!

name: City of Water Day: Boardwalk Tidal Tales
description: Celebrate City of Water Day with our experienced Urban Park Rangers. Let’s talk about intersections between land and shore at the most culturally active settlement at the mouth of the New York Harbor!

name: City of Water Day: Saltwater Fishing
description: Want to go fishing but don’t know how or where to start? Join our Urban Park Rangers for free lessons! Learn how to properly use a fishing rod. Our experienced Rangers teach the ethics of fishing and the ecology of our waterways. All equipment will be provided. Recommended for ages 8 and older.
location_name: Entrance-West Street and Greenpoint Avenue (in WNYC Transmitter Park)

name: City of Water Day: Silver Lake
description: Celebrate City of Water Day with the Urban Park Rangers. Learn how Silver Lake supports local wildlife and connects to New York City’s larger water system.
location_name: Forest Avenue and Silver Lake Park Road (in Silver Lake Park)
secondary_categories: Nature,Urban Park Rangers,City of Water Day,Climate Week NYC

name: Total Body Reset
description: This fun low-impact full-body workout includes cardio, strength and balance exercises to improve your overall fitness.Class will meet on the Greene Avenue lawn area.

name: Kids in Motion: Tie Dye
description: Join our Kids in Motion staff for a fun session of tie dye. Our team will help tie shirts in basic designs or you can try your own design. Bring your own new white T-shirts to dye. There will be a limited supply of youth-sized shirts available at the event.

name: Phoenix Community Garden Farm Stand
description: Phoenix Community Garden's Farm Stand is open for the season, every Saturday rain or shine until mid-November. Each bag contains local farm fresh fruit, salad greens, the best seasonal vegetables, and aromatics and herbs for flavor. There is sliding scale pricing. Cards, tap to pay and EBT are accepted at the register. Learn more and pre-order on the garden's website here.Pick up at Phoenix Community Garden, 24 Somers Street, between 11:00 a.m. and 3:00 p.m.

name: Community Work Day
description: Roll up your sleeves and get your hands dirty at an upcoming volunteer day!Stop by the Wyckoff Urban Farm on the fourth Saturday of every month from April-October between 11AM-1PM to to volunteer alongside our farm staff to care for our space and participate in special work projects. Learn about what we're growing, get to know our farmers, and get your hands dirty with farm projects!Tickets are not required to attend, but it's always helpful to RSVP so we can plan projects accordingly.THINGS TO KNOW:This event is family-friendly (ages 10+). We welcome individuals and families with kids ages 10 and over but children must be accompanied by an adult for the duration of their stay.Dress for the mess! Make sure to wear clothes that you don't mind getting messy in and wear close toed shoes to protect your feet.Come Prepared: We will provide all the tools and protective gear for your projects but coming prepared with a hat, long sleeved shirt, sunscreen, and bug spray is always a good idea.Facilities: Water fountain and public restrooms are available on-site. We recommend bringing snacks and a refillable water bottle.Weather: Community Work Days are rain or shine. If extreme weather is expected, we will update this page and contact those on the RSVP list to cancel.Volunteer Groups: This event is intended for individuals, families, and small groups of 5 or fewer participants. Community organizations and corporate volunteer groups are welcome to make advance reservations by emailing info@wyckoffmuseum.org to discuss scheduling options and availability.
location_name: Wyckoff Farmhouse Museum (in Fidler-Wyckoff House Park)

name: It's My Park at Hollis Playground
description: This It's My Park season, volunteer with Queens United Program For Youth Development Corporation to paint benches at Hollis Playground. We look forward to cleaning up the park with you!

name: Ongoing Outdoor Museum Exhibit: Alice's Terracotta Garden
description: Terra cotta sculpture garden featuring antique architectural ornaments from Tottenville's Atlantic Terra Cotta Company (west side lawn of Biddle House).NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to visit a unique collection of terracotta artifacts that chronicle turn-of-the-twentieth-century architectural styles common in New York City and across the United States.
location_name: H.H. Biddle House (in Conference House Park)

name: Dance in the Park: Merengue
description: Bring your energy and get ready to move at Merengue in the Park! Set against the backdrop of the waterfront and summer breeze, this lively class focuses on rhythm, connection, and fun. Participants will learn easy-to-follow steps and enjoy the fast-paced spirit of Merengue with an enthusiastic instructor leading the way. Perfect for all skill levels, this is a great opportunity to try something new or refine your moves. Come early for our 10 AM yoga class to warm up beforehand!Location: East River Picnic AreaFor inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

name: Cardio Punch
description: Cardio Punch is a total body, non-contact workout fit for all levels. Punch, hook, and kick to motivating, high-energy music that progress into heart-pumping combinations.
location_name: Boardwalk at Beach 60th Street (in Rockaway Beach and Boardwalk)

name: Bioblitz: Concrete Plant Park to Starlight Park with RJ Hawkins
description: Join RJ for a BioBlitz event that will teach Bronx residents how to document wildlife and biodiversity along the Bronx River using the iNaturalist app. Participants will take part in a guided exploration between local parks while learning about urban ecology and how citizen science can support long-term conservation and restoration efforts in NYC.

name: Bodyweight Circuit Training
description: This circuit training class combines bodyweight resistance training and conditioning to provide you with a dynamic full-body workout. Whether you are a beginner who wants to learn proper form or know your way around the gym and looking for a challenge- this class is for you! Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups and will get your heart pumping.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Chair Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in Chair Yoga, a class to be enjoyed by all- but designed for those who prefer to be seated. Experience a fun and light-hearted class that provides a seated flow. The goal is to raise your heart rate, challenge all muscle groups and improve balance while remaining seated with minimal impact on joints. Enjoy breath work to ensure you leave class feeling challenged and balanced!
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Dance Fitness
description: Dance Fitness is a creative workout that fuses dance and aerobics. The goal is to improve cardio fitness while having fun and getting a full body workout! This class is taught to music with easy-to-follow steps that will surely get your heart pumping!

name: One Book One Bronx book club
description: Come join the garden group at Bronx River Community Garden for a One Book One Bronx event! This is a weekly community space that sparks powerful conversations and celebrates diverse voices. Saturdays, May 23-August 8, registration required.

name: Tea and Meditation with Tia Colon
description: Join us on The Bronx River with Meditation Facilitator Mattia “Tia” Colón MSW, in this immersive soundbath meditation + herbal tea tasting which invites participants (kids, caregivers, elders, everyone!) into a sacred space of calmness and tranquility. Through the soothing sounds and gentle vibrations of Tibetan meditation instruments, we’ll open pathways for healing, stress relief and focus. After the meditation, we’ll gather together for an herbal tea tasting and explore the herbs growing on the Bronx River Foodway. All ages welcome! Bring your family and friends to connect with meditation and locally grown herbs. If called please bring a journal to take notes and/or write down your thoughts.Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops as well as a part of a larger City of Water Day Celebration supported by the Bronx River Alliance.

name: Indigenous Art Workshop with NAIC-NY
description: Join the the North American Indigenous Center of New York and the Bronx River Alliance for an Indigenous Art Workshop at Starlight Park!This family and kid-friendly event will offer participants an opportunity to learn traditional indigenous crafts, and gain exposure to contemporary indigenous cultures.This activity is first come, first served.This project is made possible with funds from the Statewide Community Regrants Program, a regrant program of the New York State Council on the Arts with support of the Office of the Governor and the New York State Legislature and administered by the Bronx Council on the Arts.

name: Bronx River City of Water Day Celebration
description: Join the Bronx River Alliance on Saturday July 25, from 12-4pm at Starlight Park, to celebrate City of Water Day! We’re coming together to celebrate our waterfronts and help build resilient, connected communities.There are so many ways to enjoy this free family-friendly event! Activities include:Learn about the wildlife of the Bronx River on a Guided Nature WalkHydration Station with teas foraged from the Bronx River FoodwayInteractive Community DrummingTea and Healing Sound BathFresh Produce DistributionIndigenous Art Workshop with the North American Indigenous Center of New YorkCanoe-Painting Art ProjectWeaving WorkshopCanoeing River Cleanup (registration required)And more!
secondary_categories: Best for Kids,Volunteer,Kayaking and Canoeing,City of Water Day

name: Celebrate Shorebirds
description: Love the beach? So do nesting shorebirds, like the piping plover. These tiny shorebirds fly over 700 miles to nest at Rockaway Beach every year. Join NYC Park’s Piping Plover Team and other community partners for a day of educational activities and crafts to help us better understand how we can all share the beach and support a healthy living environment. Event is for all ages, with activities geared to younger audiences.
location_name: Boardwalk at Beach 60th Street (in Rockaway Beach and Boardwalk)

name: Summer Sports Experience: Basketball Seasonal Clinics at Faber Recreation Center
description: The Summer Sports Experience programs participants receive age-appropriate instruction through organized clinics, skill stations, drills, conditioning, and live game situations led by NYC Parks Staff.
location_name: Multi-Use Room B (in Faber Pool and Park)

name: Shea Butter Grooves
description: This multi-genre dance class in celebration of joy across the African diaspora. We’ll move through the infectious rhythms of the Caribbean, the pulsing energy of Detroit-style house music, the funk and attitude of hip hop, and the feel-good grooves of reggae and R&B. Come ready to sweat, let loose, and connect with movement that honors the vibrancy and resilience of diaspora culture worldwide. All levels welcome—just bring an open heart and your energy!

name: It's My Park at Adam Clayton Powell Jr. Malls
description: This It's My Park season, volunteer with West 137th Street Block Association for Adam Clayton Powell Jr. Boulevard to spread mulch, remove litter, and support on some light weeding on West 137th Street and Adam Clayton Powell Jr. Malls. We look forward to cleaning up with you!
location_name: 125th Street & Adam Clayton Powell Malls (in Adam Clayton Powell Jr. Malls)

name: Yoga
description: This open level class is designed to awaken your mind, body and senses through controlled breathing, simple meditation, and the adoption of specific bodily postures. Please bring a mat and a lock to lock up your bag. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes,Yoga & Pilates Classes

name: Inwood Canoe Club City of Water Saturday Open House
description: City of Water Day 2026: Saturday, July 25In place of our usual Sunday Open House, join Inwood Canoe Club on SATURDAY, JULY 25 for City of Water Day, celebrating water access for everyone across the five boroughs.We will host a special Open House program in Inwood Hill Park, near the Columbia Boathouse—not at our own boathouse on the Hudson. This special version of Open House showcases the scenic waters of Muscota Marsh and Spuyten Duyvil Creek, not far from the club's original location over a hundred years ago.City of Water Open House will be held from 1pm to 3pm, on Saturday July 25, in Inwood Hill Park. In-person sign-up begins at 12:15pm. First come, first served.There will be no public programs at our boathouse that weekend. Regular Sunday Open House resumes on August 1.Día de la Ciudad del Agua 2026: Sábado 25 de julioEn lugar de nuestra habitual Puertas Abiertas en domingo, vengan el SÁBADO 25 DE JULIO para el Día de la Ciudad del Agua, que celebra el acceso al agua para todos.Tendremos un programa de Puertas Abiertas especial en el Parque Inwood Hill, en el embarcadero de Columbia, no en nuestro club. Esta versión especial de Puertas Abiertas muestra las pintorescas aguas de Muscota Marsh y Spuyten Duyvil Creek, no lejos del lugar original del club hace más de cien años.El programa de Puertas Abiertas de la Ciudad del Agua comienza a la una de la tarde y termina a las tres. La inscripción en persona comienza a las 12:15 de la tarde. Por orden de llegada.No habrá actividades públicas en nuestro club ese fin de semana. El programa de Puertas Abiertas habitual se reanuda el 1 de agosto.
location_name: Muscota Marsh (in Inwood Hill Park),West 218th Street and Indian Road (in Inwood Hill Park)

name: Basketball Clinic at Highbridge Recreation Center (Ages: 5 to 7)
description: This Instructional basketball clinic will provide an opportunity for youth, regardless of skill to have a positive basketball experience followed by league play. Participants will have the opportunity to learn and practice the fundamentals of Basketball and advance their basketball skills through a series of drills. This specific clinic for youth ages 5 and 7.

name: CANCELED: Hula Hoop Aerobics
description: his creative and fun workout fuses high-energy dance aerobics with hula hoops! Hoops are available for loaning. Participants must bring waist belts (waist trainers). Participants must bring locks to lock up their bags. Center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except; towel, water and phone.
location_name: Multi-Use Room B (in Marcus Garvey Park)
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Dance Classes,Exercise Classes

name: STRONG Nation®
description: STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more. Mats are available for those who need one. Participants must bring locks to lock up their bags. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.
location_name: Dance Room (in Constance Baker Motley Recreation Center)
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Ice Cream Flavors of the Diaspora
description: Modern ice cream production and the plants that create some of our favorite flavors are possible thanks to the brilliance of Black and Indigenous people. During this session we’ll learn about the contributions of these people alongside the stories of plants like vanilla, sorghum, sorrel, and blueberries as we create ice creams infused with their flavors. If you want to develop your ice cream making skills and plant knowledge you won’t wanna miss this session!NYC Parks GreenThumb hosts this event. We are proud to be the nation’s most extensive urban community gardening program, sustaining over 550 gardens throughout New York City. With GreenThumb’s support, community gardens are centers of environmental justice, ensuring that our city is fairer, safer, and greener for all. GreenThumb’s events are free and open to the public.

name: City of Water Day: Salt Marsh Exploration
description: Join the Rangers for a special City of Water Day celebration with a guided walk along the Muscota Marsh. Discover the area’s biodiversity as the Rangers highlight the birds, mammals, and other wildlife that call this unique habitat home
location_name: West 218th Street and Indian Road (in Inwood Hill Park)
secondary_categories: Nature,Urban Park Rangers,City of Water Day,Climate Week NYC

name: Soccer Skills with New York Football Academy
description: Lace up and get ready to move! Youth will meet at the farm before heading to St. Nicholas Playground North for a fun soccer skills session with NYFA. Kids will build confidence, practice teamwork, and sharpen their footwork through games, exercises, and plenty of movement. All attendees must fill out the Saturdays on the Farm registration and complete the farm waiver: https://www.harlemgrown.org/saturdays-on-the-farm

name: Paddle and Pick Up at Starlight Park
description: Join the Bronx River Alliance for a Paddle and Pick Up as part of our City of Water Day Celebration!This guided canoeing river clean up will allow participants to enjoy the river and protect local wildlife at the same time!Participants will be given gloves, grabbers, and trash bags or buckets, to safely remove floatable litter from the Bronx River.Minors above the age of 10 are welcome to participate, but MUST be accompanied by an adult. All minors must have an adult in their boat, and have a waiver signed by a legal guardian.Previous canoeing experience is strongly recommended for this activity. Advanced registration is required to participate.

name: It's My Park at Hunter's Point South Park
description: This It's My Park season, volunteer with Hunters Point Parks Conservancy to beautify Hunter's Point South Park. Remove plastic and other trash from the riprap along the edge of the park. Due to the type of activity performed during this project, volunteers must be aged 18 and older.

name: Park Open Studio
description: Led by a local teaching artist, this drop-in class invites participants to explore creativity through hands-on artmaking under the shade of Scrabble Plaza’s trees. Past mediums have included plein-air drawing, printmaking, cyanotypes, and more. All materials are provided by the Washington Square Park Conservancy.
location_name: Northwest Corner Seating Area (in Washington Square Park)

name: CANCELLED: Cooking on the Farm
description: Please note: this event has been cancelled.Experience the flavors of Randall’s Island Park Alliance’s Urban Farm through this free, interactive cooking demonstration! Join an experienced chef in the Urban Farm Kitchen for an afternoon of farm-to-table fun. After learning about the cultural significance of the recipe and its freshly harvested ingredients, participants will work together to chop, crush, and stir fresh ingredients, and enjoy a small plate of the finished dish. Guests will leave with new knowledge of how to use seasonal produce to cook a healthy and delicious meal.Registration is required. The link will open 2 weeks before the workshop.Location: Urban FarmFor inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

name: Tapestry Weaving Workshop with Amirtha Arasu
description: Join Amirtha for a workshop that aims to teach the basics of tapestry weaving and how to use a simple frame loom. Participants will be introduced to different materials and will be able to take a hand-made loom home after the workshop, so you can keep on weaving! Fibers will also be provided.Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops.

name: From Plant to Paper with Sana Ashraf
description: Join us on the Bronx River with Sana Ashraf for plant-based paper making! While paper products today are largely mass produced, the techniques and guiding methodology are still rooted in its handmade origins. As such, this is a craft that can be easily adapted to a variety of materials, without investing in expensive equipment, and with a little creativity allows us to utilize and upcycle materials available in the plant world, our recycling bins, and even kitchen scraps intended for the compost pile.The session will involve a guided gathering of plant materials* from Concrete Plant Park, which will then be used to implement the process of plant based paper making.*The bulk of this will be focused on plants whose removal benefits the ongoing weeding and other stewardship works.Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops.

name: Bloom to the Beat: A Bronx River Sound & Flower Experience with Zinzi Gonzalez
description: Bloom to the Beat is an interactive outdoor workshop with Zinzi Gonzalez where participants explore the Bronx River through sound, storytelling, and floral design. Guests will listen to curated vinyl-inspired playlists rooted in Bronx and NYC music culture while creating their own small floral arrangements inspired by the river’s natural elements—growth, flow, and resilience.

name: Celebrate Marine Park's history
description: On City of Water Day find out how the creation of Marine Park highlighst how people have always fought for access to water for recreation and inspiration. Learn about the remarkable transformations that created the largest park in Brooklyn. Thomas J. Campanella, Historian-in-Residence of the NYC Parks Department, author of Brooklyn: The Once and Future City, will be talking about the history of Marine Park. Sponsored by the Salt Marsh Alliance.
location_name: Salt Marsh Nature Center (in Marine Park)

name: Basketball Clinic at Highbridge Recreation Center (Ages: 8 to 12)
description: This Instructional basketball clinic will provide an opportunity for youth, regardless of skill to have a positive basketball experience followed by league play. Participants will have the opportunity to learn and practice the fundamentals of Basketball and advance their basketball skills through a series of drills. This specific clinic for youth ages 8 and 12.

name: Public Gallery Tour
description: Solo and group exhibitions at Glyndor Gallery explore the dynamic relationships between nature, culture, and site. When exhibitions are on view in the spring, summer, and fall, Gallery Greeters lead public exhibition tours on Thursdays and Saturdays at 2PM. Visitors can expect an in-depth look at artworks on view in Glyndor Gallery and in the Sunroom Project Space, showcasing the work of both emerging and established artists in the unique context of a public garden. Each tour lasts approximately 45 minutes. Drop-in event; no registration required. Today’s tour features current exhibitions. Questions? Please email us at information@wavehill.org or call 718.549.3200 x251. Glyndor Gallery is wheelchair-accessible. There is an accessible, ground-level entrance at the front of the building with an elevator that provides access to the gallery level. The Sunroom Project Space can be accessed with an ADA-compliant ramp. The restroom on the gallery level is all-gender and ADA-compliant.

name: Worms at Work: Compost Sifting & Soil Discovery
description: Get your hands in the soil and meet the tiny workers helping our farm grow! Participants will sift compost, explore worm bins, and learn how food scraps turn into rich, healthy soil. Perfect for curious kids, families, and anyone who loves a little garden science. All attendees must fill out the Saturdays on the Farm registration and complete the farm waiver: https://www.harlemgrown.org/saturdays-on-the-farm

name: Summer on the Hudson: Vinyl Nights
description: Vinyl Nights presents a vibrant, inclusive dance party and celebration of “Everything New York.” Dance to the hits that shaped NYC & experience what it was like to party in the 70’s-90’s when the DJ’s played strictly vinyl, including disco, funk, soul, house, boogie, Latin, freestyle & 80’s & 90’s hip hop, presented by Rebecca Lynn.July 25 featuring Operator Emz & DJ Misbehaviour!

name: Hudson Classical Theater Company presents: The Dancing Men
description: The Hudson Classical Theater Company Presents The Dancing Men: A Sherlock Holmes Mystery written by Susane Lee, adapted from the short story by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, and directed by Susane Lee and Nicholas Martin-Smith.Hilton Cubitt seeks the help of Sherlock Holmes when he comes across a series of mysterious stick figure drawings resembling “dancing men” about his garden. These drawings are linked to his wife, Elsie Patrick, an American woman who harbors a secret past. Holmes needs to decipher these "dancing men" before tragedy strikes. The Dancing Men runs July 23rd - August 16th. All shows are Thursdays to Sundays at 6:30pm. No reservations--arrive by 6:15pm for a good seat. Cushions provided. Hudson Classical Theater Company Shows are “Pay What You Can.” Audience donations help pay for artists stipends and production costs. Directions to The Soldiers’ & Sailors’ Monument:Subways: Take the 1 to West 86 or West 96, or take the 2 to West 96. Walk west towards Riverside Park. You can enter the park at West 89th street or take the entrance at West 91st Street. You will see the Soldiers’ and Sailors’ Monument. Take the path to the right of the monument (not the one closest to the river). Walk around the path and you will be BEHIND the monument where audiences will be seated.
location_name: Soldiers' and Sailors' Monument (in Riverside Park)

name: RTA Summer Sunset Concert Series featuring Richard Baratta
description: Waterfront sunset concerts sponsored by the Riverside Tennis Association. No performance on July 4th. June 20 - Ron McClure & Friends June 27 - Morrisania Band Project July 11 - The Afro-LatineersJuly 18 - The Slim Kings July 25 - Richard Baratta August 1 - The BrooklynersAugust 8 - DurieuxRichard Baratta has worn many hats over the decades: master drummer, Grammy nominee, film producer, musician, but his latest headwear takes us down a nostalgic road of classic rock and soul recordings from the mid to late 1960’s. Baratta returned to his original love, music, after a very successful 35-year career in the film business. With over 50 years of experience as a jazz drummer he enjoys the free flowing innovative sensibility that he applies to his work in the film industry. As 30 year member of the Directors Guild of America, as well as the Producers Guild of America, Baratta has worked on many Academy Award nominated films, including Donnie Brasco, The Wolf Of Wall Street, The Irishman, Joker, and othersSince 2020 Baratta has recorded five projects, most notably the album Music in Film featuring Grammy nominated track Chopsticks, with his current working band of Vincent Herring, Bill O’Connell, Paul Bollenback, Michael Goetz and Paul Rossman. Music In Film The Sequel, is a similar follow up, building and exploring more film compositions. Off The Charts, the fourth project was a critically acclaimed, audience favorite, looking back at some of the classic B- sides of Jazz from the 1970’s, with musicians John Patitucci, Jerry Bergonzi, David Kikoski, and Paul Rossman. All three of these recording spent many weeks on Jazz Week, the definitive guide to national airplay.

name: Wheel Fun Rentals Celebrates America's 250th with Patriotic LED Bike Ride
description: Wheel Fun Rentals at Boathouse Row is lighting up summer nights in patriotic style as it celebrates America's 250th anniversary with special Red, White & Blue LED Illuminated Surrey Night Rides. Patriotic Surrey Nights Rides are available now and will run through Labor Day Weekend at their Bensonhurst Park and FDR Boardwalk at Sand Lane bike rental locations.For the summer-long celebration, Wheel Fun Rentals has transformed its popular LED-illuminated specialty bikes into dazzling patriotic attractions featuring custom red, white, and blue lighting. Families, couples, and friends can cruise along two of New York City's scenic waterfronts while celebrating America's historic 250th birthday.On July 4th, guests can make the holiday even more memorable by riding to waterfront viewing areas for spectacular fireworks displays. Launching from Bensonhurst Park in Brooklyn, riders can enjoy the festive atmosphere along the waterfront bike path with views across New York Harbor. On Staten Island, guests can pedal along the FDR Boardwalk, where the waterfront provides a scenic setting to take in the evening's Independence Day celebrations and fireworks over the harbor.Night Rides are first-come, first-served and available Friday – Sundays and holidays beginning at sunset until 10pm, last 1-hour rental goes out at 9pm. Pricing is $29 for a Single Surrey (seats up to 3 adults and 2 small children), and $39 for a Double Surrey (seats up to 6 adults and 2 small children).For additional location information, current hours of operation, and to learn more about Wheel Fun Rentals available recreational rental options, visit wheelfunrentals.com/NYC.
location_name: Meadow Lake North (in Flushing Meadows Corona Park),Sand Lane and Father Capadanno Boulevard (in Franklin D. Roosevelt Boardwalk and Beach),Marine Park,Bensonhurst Park,David Dinkins Circle (in Flushing Meadows Corona Park)
address: Flushing Meadows Corona Park, Franklin D. Roosevelt Boardwalk and Beach, Marine Park, Bensonhurst Park
